objective: Test a direct horizon-partitioned learner rather than another correction on the saturated cross-group surface.
evidence: The GEFCom 2012 wind solution trained separate models for forecast-time slots.
BARAM has one daily issue cycle with exact lead hours +12 through +35.
Prior lead-phase work partitioned an ExtraTrees cross-group member, not the strongest direct CatBoost learner.
constraints: keep the base feature set and MAE loss unchanged
select iterations and calibration only on Q2
compare against both the exact current surface and the monolithic CatBoost parent
do not emit a submission from the structural diagnostic
